item.title: Mithras: Master of Mystery - Ronald Hutton
item.description: The cult of Mithras was by far the most famous of the mystery religions of the Roman Empire: private societies of worshippers devoted to a…

item.title: Hecate: Mistress of Magic - Ronald Hutton
item.description: Hecate started as the ruling goddess of the Asian region of Caria, and got taken over by the Greeks as the only one able to operate in every realm…

item.title: The Great God Pan: Lord of the Wild - Ronald Hutton
item.description: Pan started as a shepherds’ god in a wild and backward area of Greece, but became one of the best-known in the Greek and Roman world. This was…

item.title: Modern Pagan Witchcraft - Ronald Hutton
item.description: This lecture was recorded by Ronald Hutton on 14th May 2025 at Barnard's Inn Hall, London. Ronald is the Gresham Professor of Divinity. He is…

item.description: Watch the Q&A session here: https://youtu.be/qz9a4zXIFz0 The ancient Druids have long represented some of the most striking and controversial…

item.title: What is Modern Paganism? - Ronald Hutton
item.description: What is modern Paganism, and how does it relate to witchcraft, Druidry and other phenomena? This lecture is designed to answer that question, and in…

item.title: Witch-Hunting in European and World History - Ronald Hutton
item.description: This lecture confronts the worldwide phenomenon of the persecution of suspected witches, now a serious, contemporary problem condemned by the UN in…

item.title: The Western Magical Tradition - Ronald Hutton
item.description: This lecture makes a survey of learned ceremonial magic in Europe throughout history and demonstrates that both of the customary claims made for it…

item.title: Ritual Nudity in History and Religion - Ronald Hutton
item.description: This lecture looks at the role played by nudity in European religion and magic from ancient times to the present, with some reference to a global…

item.title: Dragons: A History - Ronald Hutton
item.description: Why have people believed in dragons, and what were they actually? Is there a difference between Western and Eastern dragons, in a global…
